content.full_text:
COUNTY COUNSEL
108 COURT STREET • JACKSON, CA 95642-2379 • (209) 223-6366 • FAX (209) 223-0499

Dec 20 4 02 PM '90

December 19, 1990


John Wallace, Esq., Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, #800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest/Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Wallace:

As the county counsel for Amador County, I have been asked to
furnish an opinion as to whether or not two members of the board of
supervisors separately have conflicts of interest and are
disqualified from participating in the decision on an appeal from
the granting of a use permit by the Amador County planning
commission. I believe I also have a potential conflict of interest
and therefore ask the FPPC for formal written advice pursuant to
subsection 18329(b) of title 2 of the California Regulatory Code on
all three of the potential conflicts. I am authorized by the
supervisors to make this request.

Issue Before the Board of Supervisors

Mr. Kenneth Deaver applied to the County for a use permit for a bed
and breakfast inn on a 40-acre parcel on Steiner Road in Amador
County ("Deaver parcel" hereinafter). The planning commission
granted the use permit and it has been appealed to the board of
supervisors.

Steiner Road lies in the Shenandoah Valley of Amador County, a
region predominantly devoted to the growing of wine grapes. Along
Steiner Road in the immediate vicinity of the Deaver parcel are
four wineries, two of which are Santino Winery and Spinetta Winery.
The chief executive officer of Santino Winery is Scott Hardy who
also lives and operates a vineyard on Steiner Road. The owner of
Spinetta Winery is Charles Spinetta. Mr. Spinetta also operates
from his winery a wine distribution company and has an adjoining
vineyard. On the Deaver parcel is a house, where the bed and
breakfast operation would take place, and a refurbished building
originally built as a winery and now used to warehouse cased wine
not owned by Mr. Deaver.

Mr. Deaver has a variety of business interests including the
investing in real property with partners. Supervisor Tim Davenport
is an equal co-owner with Mr. Deaver on five parcels of property
located in Amador, El Dorado and Tuolumne Counties. None is
remotely close to the Deaver parcel. The equity in the five
parcels is in excess of $100,000. Supervisor Davenport owns no
interest in the Deaver parcel.

Mr. Harvey and Mr. Spinetta among many others are vehemently
opposed to the proposed use of the Beaver parcel for reasons
centering around (a) an expected adverse impact caused by the bed
and breakfast inn on their vineyard operations and (b) increased
traffic on Steiner Road. They argue that vineyard operations such
as sulphuring and other farming practices some of which are
customarily done at night will be incompatible with a bed and
breakfast inn and ultimately will curtail some farming practices of
the grape growers increasing their costs and winery operators'
costs. They believe increased traffic from Mr. Beaver's proposed
use will be bad for their homes and their wineries.

I am a general partner in two vineyards in the Shenandoah Valley
one of which is on Steiner Road about one-quarter mile away from
the Beaver parcel. My partner and I have no position regarding the
bed and breakfast operation. However, part of the partnerships'
operation includes having wine made from our grapes for sale to the
public. Cases of our wine are warehoused at Mr. Beaver's warehouse
on the Beaver parcel for which we pay several hundred dollars a
year to Mr. Beaver. Also, the partnerships sell grapes to Santino
Winery and receive more than ten thousand dollars per year
therefrom. The partnerships have employed Mr. Spinetta to
distribute their wine, with revenue to the partnerships of several
thousand dollars yearly.

In addition, another supervisor, Mr. Tom Bamert, is a director and
a shareholder (about 3%) of a local bank. Mr. Beaver is a director
and a shareholder of the same bank. Mr. Beaver is a borrower and
a depositor of significant proportions at the local bank. He
originally bought the Beaver parcel from the bank. It is not known
if Mr. Beaver has any debt with the bank secured by the Beaver
parcel. The second supervisor, Mr. Bamert, may have a conflict of
interest because of his and Mr. Beaver's mutual interest in the
bank.

Because of my partnerships' business interests with Messrs.
Beaver, Spinetta, and Harvey (on behalf of Santino Winery), I
believe that I have a conflict of interest and should recuse myself
from participating in the appeal and on the issuing of opinions
regarding possible conflicts of interest of the two supervisors.
I have one deputy and I do not wish to place him in the position of
writing an opinion regarding my own potential conflict of interest.

Therefore, I seek formal written advice on the following three
questions:

1.    Do I as county counsel have a potential conflict of
interest so as to preclude me from issuing an opinion as to the
supervisors' possible conflicts of interest and otherwise
participating in the appeal?

2.    Does Supervisor Davenport have a potential conflict of
interest on Mr. Beaver's appeal?

3.     Does Supervisor Bamert have a potential conflict of
interest on Mr. Deaver's appeal?

Thank you very much (in advance) for providing formal written
advice on these issues. I request that your advice be forthcoming
as soon as possible because the appeal will be heard in January.

Yours very truly,

[signature]

JOHN F. HAHN
County Counsel

JFH/p
